explicit_text: I was born and raised in Mexico, where my father had a small business as the town's milkman. My family lived there for many years, and I helped my dad with his work. Although my mother was American, she stayed mostly in the United States after she got sick during her last pregnancy. I lived in Mexico until I was able to cross the border and join my family in the U.S., but I never went back to live there after that.
implicit_text: I was born and raised in a country south of the U.S. border, where my father had a small business as the town's milkman. I lived there until I was able to cross the border and join my family in the northern neighbor, but I never went back to live there after that.

explicit_text: I came to the United States because my mother was American, and it was allowed for children of an American mother and Mexican father to become U.S. citizens. My younger brother was even born in the U.S. after my grandma took my mother there for medical reasons. Eventually, my mother brought the rest of the family over, and I lived in Los Angeles with my grandma, mom, and siblings. Later in life, after my ex retired, we moved to Utah where I took English classes to improve myself.

explicit_text: After my first attempt to cross the border failed, I was sent back to Tijuana, Mexico, where I stayed with my brother and sister-in-law for a few months. It was a difficult time because I had to wait before I could get a three-day pass to cross into the United States again. Tijuana was the place I lived right before finally joining my family in Los Angeles.

explicit_text: Once I was able to cross the border, I lived in Los Angeles, California with my grandma, mother, sister, and two brothers. This was my first home in the United States, and I never returned to live in Mexico after moving there. In Los Angeles, I got married, raised five children, and built my family life.

explicit_text: After my ex-husband retired, we moved to Utah to start a new chapter in our lives. It was here that my friend Ceci told me about an English class, which I decided to take despite the challenges. The teacher and other students were very patient and helpful, and I enjoyed the class. Living in Utah has been a positive experience as I continue to maintain good relationships with my family and friends.
